question: In an MPLS topology with multiple VPN services between two points, which statements regarding label propagation are true?
options: The service label (per LSP) stays the same through the entire path
The transport labels are identical for all services along each segment of the path
The service labels are identical for all services along each segment of the path
The transport label (per LSP) stays the same through the entire path
Both the service and the transport label are 'random' and vary at each hop
correct_answers: The service label (per LSP) stays the same through the entire path
The transport labels are identical for all services along each segment of the path

question: Regarding a Nokia router, what is the purpose of the command: 'tools perform router mpls cspf...'?
options: It forces the resignalling of an LSP
It checks whether a particular LSP is possible
correct_answers: It checks whether a particular LSP is possible

question: Identify the restriction(s) on the propagation of Traffic Engineering (TE) properties in a network.
options: All routers must be Opaque capable
All routers must have Traffic Engineering enabled
correct_answers: All routers must be Opaque capable

question: In which direction(s) are labels signaled and used within an MPLS network?
options: None of the other answers is correct

question: Which parts of a router are responsible for signaling and using MPLS labels?
options: Labels are signaled via the management plane
Labels are signaled via the data plane
Labels are used by the control plane
Labels are signaled via the control plane
Labels are used by the data plane
correct_answers: Labels are signaled via the control plane
Labels are used by the data plane

question: Which specific alert is used in connection with MPLS LSPs?
Which objects or database entries are involved in maintaining an RSVP session?
Which of the following are signaled only end-to-end in an MPLS topology rather than formed from a chain of point-to-point sessions?
Which of the following is true about using OAM ping and OAM trace commands on a Nokia router?
options: The requests use the LSP and the replies use plain IP
The replies use the LSP and the requests use plain IP
OAM trace uses an Alert label, OAM ping does not
They both always show all LSR hops
None of the other answers is correct
correct_answers: The requests use the LSP and the replies use plain IP

question: Which labels can be popped by the egress router without performing a lookup?
Which protocol is used to signal MPLS transport labels?
What terms define a Forwarding Equivalence Class (FEC)?
options: Packets forwarded in the same manner
Packets forwarded over the same path
Packets forwarded with the same forwarding treatment
Packets always forwarded with both a service and a transport label
Packets always forwarded with either a service or a transport label but not both
correct_answers: Packets forwarded in the same manner
Packets forwarded over the same path
Packets forwarded with the same forwarding treatment

question: On a Nokia LDP router, which labels received from peers are discarded and not entered into the Label Information Base (LIB)?
options: Labels that don't match the import policy are discarded
Labels must match both the import and export policy to be retained
Labels that don't match the export policy are discarded
Labels that lead towards the IGP egress path are discarded

question: An MPLS router is signaled a label of 3 (implicit null) from its downstream router. What action will it take when receiving a data packet for this LSP?
options: Perform label swapping and transmit with label 3
Push the label of 3 and transmit the packet
Extract the data packet to the CPM for OAM options
Pop the outer label and forward the data packet to the next-hop
correct_answers: Pop the outer label and forward the data packet to the next-hop

question: What is the maximum number of labels in the label stack that a Label Switch Router (LSR) will modify for MPLS VPN frames?
Match the following MPLS special use labels to their standard decimal values.
In an LDP-over-RSVP tunnel scenario, which labels remain identical across all hops?
options: All labels change at some point
correct_answers: All labels change at some point

question: If Link LDP is used between R1, R2, and R3, where R1 has two links to R2 and one link to R3, how many Link LDP sessions will R2 have?
Which statement describes packets egressing a CE router directly connected to a PE in an MPLS VPN environment?
options: The packets have one outer transport label and one inner service label
The packets have one inner transport label and one outer service label
There is not enough information to answer the question

question: Match the RSVP message types to their behavior during LSP teardown or signaling.
options: Sent from head end to tail end, intercepted by each router along the path.
Sent from the head end towards the tail end when an operator shuts down the LSP.
A message type used to signal errors in the path.

correct_answers.A: Sent from head end to tail end, intercepted by each router along the path.
correct_answers.B: A message type used to signal errors in the path.
correct_answers.C: Sent from the head end towards the tail end when an operator shuts down the LSP.
